LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST, THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE DETERMINATION OF THE MERITS OF THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION REQUIRES FURTHER FACTUAL DEVELOPMENT AND CANNOT BE RESOLVED AT THE PLEADING STAGE. THE COMPLAINT AND JUDICIALLY NOTICEABLE MATERIALS DO NOT SHOW THAT PROPOSITION Q VIOLATES PROPOSITION 218, SECTION 17041.5 OR THE SINGLE SUBJECT RULE NOR HAS PLAINTIFF SHOWN THAT IT CAN ALLEGE SUFFICIENT FACTS TO DO SO. ORDER SIGNED. (302/HEK) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Edwin P. Antolin, Amy L. Silverstein and Johanna W. Roberts of Silverstein & Pomerantz (593.3505) for plaintiff and responding party; James M. Emery of the Office of the City Attorney (554.4261) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: overruled as to the second cause of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the first, third and fourth causes of action. The determination of the merits of the second cause of action requires further factual development and cannot be resolved at the pleading stage. The complaint and judicially noticeable materials do not show that Proposition Q violates Proposition 218, section 17041.5 or the single subject rule nor has plaintiff shown that it can allege sufficient facts to do so. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-22-2011 Continued To Dec-12-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued to December 12, 2011 per the stipulation and order. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Sonia Wong, CSR #11288
